Ad Text:
Accenture is a global professional services company with leading capabilities in digital, cloud and security. Find out more about us at accenture.com.


Responsibilities:
 

Data Architecture and Modeling:

Design and implement scalable data architecture to support the organization's data needs.

Develop and maintain data models for efficient storage, retrieval, and analysis.
 

ETL Development:

Create and optimize Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) processes to ensure efficient data movement and integration.

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather and understand data requirements.
 

Data Governance:

Establish and enforce data governance policies and procedures to ensure data quality, integrity, and compliance with regulatory requirements.

Define and implement data standards, naming conventions, and data lineage documentation.
 

Data Security:

Implement and maintain robust data security measures to protect sensitive information.

Collaborate with the IT security team to ensure compliance with data protection regulations.
 

Data Quality Assurance:

Develop and execute data quality checks and validation processes to identify and resolve data discrepancies.

Implement monitoring solutions to proactively address data quality issues.
 

Collaboration and Communication:

Work closely with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver solutions.

Communicate effectively with non-technical stakeholders, translating complex technical concepts into understandable terms.

 

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.

Proven experience as a Data Engineer with a focus on data governance.

Strong proficiency in SQL, Python, or other relevant programming languages.

In-depth knowledge of data modeling, ETL processes, and database management systems.

Familiarity with data governance tools and frameworks.

Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills.

Effective communication and collaboration skills.

Long Term Contract

Our client is a Federal Government agency that delivers a wide range of essential payments and services across health, social benefits, child support, childcare, education etc  

Role Description

The IMS COBOL Developer will be responsible for designing, developing, testing and maintaining enterprise applications running on IBM mainframe environments - using COBOL in conjunction with IMS (Information Management System).

This role ensures the stability, performance, and scalability of critical business systems by delivering highquality code, adhering to industry best practices and following organisational standards.
 
Responsibilities:

Write, modify, and optimise COBOL programs - integrated with IMS databases and transaction management.
Collaborate with business analysts to translate requirements into technical specifications.
Conduct unit, integration and regression testing to ensure system reliability.
Monitor and improve application efficiency; ensuring minimal downtime.
Provide ongoing support, troubleshoot issues and implement enhancements.
Maintain clear technical documentation for code, processes and system configurations.
Support data migration project and, subsequently, application code transformation
Develop strategy to ensure high quality code and long term maintenance of a product's code base
Provide specialist technical advice on software development and maintenance
Ensure compliance with endorsed software development methodologies
Investigate and resolve highly complex ICT issues
Lead a positive risk management culture
Skills and Experience: 

Proficiency in COBOL, IMS DB/DC, JCL, and mainframe utilities
Experience across the full lifecycle of development - design, development, configuration, integration, testing, deployment etc
Experience in testing essential
Strong analytical and troubleshooting, problem solving skills
Ability to engage with a range of stakeholders
Ability to attain NV1 security clearance (Baseline security clearance is a minimum)
Consultant: Colin Massey 

Keywords: Government, mainframe, IBM, COBOL, IMS DB/DC, JCL, software, development, baseline security clearance, NV1 security clearance

Randstad Digital (Formerly Finite Recruitment)

Randstad Digital was announced on August 30, 2023, under the umbrella of Randstad, the world's largest talent company and a partner of choice to clients. Our 46,000 employees around the world make a positive impact on society by helping people to realize their true potential throughout their working life. In 2022, in our 39 markets, we helped more than 2 million people find a job that feels good and advised over 230,000 clients on their talent needs.


Role:
As a Senior Engineer, you will be the bridge between complex systems and actionable solutions. You won't just be sitting behind a screen; you will be onsite with clients, facilitating workshops to build high-quality digital models.




Your key responsibilities include:


Training and supporting client personnel to master the platform.
Creating digital models, identifying system behaviours, and generating requirements and test cases.
Leading business process reviews, requirements analysis, and maintaining traceability from business goals to engineering artefacts.
Acting as the "voice of the client" internally to help shape our platform's future capabilities.

Skills and expereince required:

10+ years as an Engineer in technical or systems-focused roles.
Experience within Defence or Federal Government environments.
Familiarity with MBSE tools, modelling languages, or digital engineering.
Exposure to CAD, simulation, analytics, or game design tools.

Please hit the apply button or for more information contact Simmi from Randstad Digital on 0425763872.


At Randstad, we are passionate about providing equal employment opportunities and embracing diversity to the benefit of all. We actively encourage applications from any background.

About Reach Robotics: 

Our vision is to enable inspection and intervention in places humans can't go. We do this by creating advanced manipulator and perception systems for harsh environments. Our military-grade robotic arms and sensors are pushing the boundaries of what’s possible for clients in industries such as Defence, Offshore Energy, Marine Science and more. 

What You’ll Do:

Lead the design, implementation, and deployment of control algorithms for subsea robotic manipulators and accessories.

Develop intuitive user interfaces for the display of information and real-time control of remotely operated robotic systems.

Collaborate with an interdisciplinary team of mechanical / electrical / software engineers and researchers to build the next generation of underwater electric manipulators and sensors.

Take ownership for the coordination of robotics projects including stakeholder engagement, requirements definition, and roadmap development.

Collaborate with the New Product Introduction (NPI) team to develop robust validation and verification methodologies.

Required Qualifications:

Minimum 5 years of experience writing scalable production ready software applications.

Minimum 2 years of experience deploying software on embedded Linux platforms such as Nvidia Jetson.

Understanding of under lying physics principles for robotic actuators, manipulators and/or underwater vehicles.

Bachelor's degree in computer science, software engineering, mechatronic engineering, or equivalent.

Excellent organisational skills and a positive “can do” attitude with a willingness to do what is required to get the job done.

Preferred Qualifications:

Experience writing Electron based user interface applications.

Experience configuring Linux device tree and other kernel drivers.

Software development on Nvidia Jetson devices with CUDA acceleration.

Background in embedded C/C++ software development on STM32 or similar platforms.

Experience with communications protocols including RS-485/232, UDP/TCP.

Desire to grow into a lead role with the responsibility for line management of engineers.

Demonstrated experience deploying motion planning, sensor fusion, machine vision, or machine learning algorithms on edge hardware.

What We Offer:

Supportive team environment.

Diverse and collaborative workplace culture.

Competitive salary based on your experience.

Professional learning and development opportunities.

Role growth driven by your degree of motivation and skills.

Opportunity to work collaboratively with external research organisations and institutions.

Reach Robotics is an equal‑opportunity employer. If you’re passionate about tackling meaningful challenges and making a real impact, we’d love to hear from you.

Please note that Reach Robotics is managing this hiring campaign internally and will not be accepting submissions from recruitment agencies for this role.

Job Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer - Inspire & Create (AU Remote)
Ad Text:
Company Description


Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.

Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora,你好, hallo, vítejte!

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point. 

Where and how you can work

Our flagship campus is in Sydney, with a second campus in Melbourne and co-working spaces in Brisbane, Perth, Adelaide, and Auckland, NZ. You have flexibility in how and where you work — whether that's from one of our spaces, from home, or a mix of both. This role is remote-friendly within Australia, so you can choose the setup that empowers you and your team to do your best work.

Job Description


Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.

Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora, 你好, hallo, vítejte!

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point.

About the team

We're Canva Grow, one of Canva’s newest product groups, born from the acquisition of MagicBrief. Our mission is to build the surface inside Canva that brings creativity, data, and insights together - helping businesses grow through better advertising.

Advertising, when done well, connects people with things they love. Our goal is to help every business create ads that are effective, creative, and respectful of attention. We’re scaling fast, with the agility of a startup and the backing of Canva’s global platform.

You'll be joining the Inspire & Create team, leading product development at the intersection of publishing systems, creative performance, and integrated ad platforms. But your work won’t be constrained to a single team. As our first MLE in Grow we will be looking to you to inform the full feedback loop across the whole group - your work will impact the entire customer journey from Inspire → Create → Publish → Insights → Recommend → Inspire.

What you’d be doing in this role:

As Canva scales change continues to be part of our DNA. But we like to think that's all part of the fun. So this will give you the flavour of the type of things you'll be working on when you start, but this will likely evolve.

At the moment, this role is focused on:

Building, testing, and shipping ML-powered features that enhance our ad generation experiences

Designing scalable ML pipelines in collaboration with our platform teams

Working closely with PMs, designers and engineers to align on user needs and product direction

Running experiments to validate assumptions and improve model effectiveness

Contributing to shared ML infrastructure and best practices at Canva

Keeping up with state-of-the-art developments and exploring ways to apply them at scale

You're probably a match if

You’re a highly skilled machine learning engineer with a proven track record of delivering ML-powered product features in production.

You bring deep technical expertise across the ML lifecycle - from data wrangling and model development to evaluation, deployment, and monitoring.

You’re comfortable operating independently while collaborating deeply with cross-functional teams, and you’re motivated by user impact and product outcomes.

You have demonstrated experience building and deploying ML-powered features in production environments.

You have proficiency with Python and frameworks like TensorFlow or PyTorch.

You are skilled across the ML lifecycle: data processing, model training, evaluation, deployment, and monitoring.

You have proven ability to work independently while collaborating cross-functionally with product, design, and data.

You have experience with Generative AI (GenAI): LLMs, diffusion models, prompt engineering, RAG, or fine-tuning.

You have familiarity with GenAI platforms (e.g. OpenAI, Hugging Face, Anthropic) and embedding-based systems.

You have a strong product mindset - prioritises ML solutions that improve user experience and product outcomes.

You are committed to scalable, maintainable ML systems with clear metrics and impact tracking.

You are passionate about ethical ML and responsible data usage, especially in learning environments.

You enjoy mentoring and sharing best practices within the team.

Additional Information


Don't tick all the boxes? Don't worry about that - nobody does!  

We’d still love to hear from you! At Canva, we know that great engineers come from a variety of backgrounds, and we value passion, curiosity, and a willingness to learn just as much as specific experience. If you're excited about this role but don’t tick every box, we encourage you to apply, you might a great fit in ways you didn’t expect!

What's in it for you?

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a stack of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Here's a taste of what's on offer:

Equity packages - we want our success to be yours too
Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers
An annual Vibe & Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup & more
Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

Check out  lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know

We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

All interviews are conducted virtually

Job Title: AI Design Specialist (6 Month Contract) Sydney
Ad Text:
Job Description


Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.

Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora, 你好, hallo, vítejte!

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point.

Where and how you can work

Alongside our global campuses, we also collaborate with talented contractors and freelancers from all over the world to help us achieve our crazy big goals. While we support flexible, remote-friendly ways of working, for this role we’re focusing on Sydney so we can best support our teams and keep collaboration flowing.

This is a 6-month engagement working full time hours. 

What you'd be doing
As Canva scales, change continues to be part of our DNA — and nowhere is that more exciting than in AI. We’re building world-leading visual AI, and this role plays a critical part in making that happen.

You’ll sit at the intersection of design, data, and AI research, bringing world-class visual judgment into the model training and evaluation loop. In a Core Research environment, your work will materially strengthen the feedback systems that push our models toward premium, intuitive, and globally competitive performance.

At the moment, this role is focused on:

• Annotating and labeling large volumes of visual data — including images, UI layouts, compositions, graphics, and photography — with high precision
• Evaluating AI-generated outputs for aesthetic quality, composition, accessibility, usability, and brand alignment
• Developing visual scoring rubrics and structured evaluation frameworks to guide model improvement
• Identifying patterns, failure modes, and edge cases to help improve model performance
• Translating nuanced design critique into structured, actionable feedback for ML engineers and research scientists
• Using generative AI tools to prototype workflows, test hypotheses, and creatively explore model behaviors
• Contributing to the development of taxonomies for visual styles, attributes, and design patterns
• Maintaining high standards of data quality and documentation across annotation projects

You're probably a match if

• You have strong design fundamentals — composition, color theory, typography, layout, hierarchy, and storytelling are second nature to you
• You have a portfolio that demonstrates visual excellence across graphic design, product design, UI/UX, illustration, or related domains
• You’re familiar with generative AI tools and understand how training data and annotation influence model behavior
• You can evaluate visual outputs with precision and clearly articulate structured, repeatable feedback
• You’re comfortable working in documentation-heavy, cross-functional environments
• You’re excited by the idea of shaping how AI models “see” and “create”

Bonus points if you have:

• Experience with Figma, Adobe Creative Cloud, or 3D tools
• Prior annotation, data labeling, or ML operations experience
• Basic familiarity with Python, prompt engineering, or dataset tooling
• Knowledge of accessibility guidelines (e.g. WCAG), usability heuristics, or user research 

About the team
This role sits within Content Studio in the Content & Discovery Supergroup — the team focused on empowering the world through world-class content.

We combine creativity, research, and technology to ensure Canva delivers visually outstanding, accessible, and globally relevant design experiences. As Canva accelerates its AI capabilities, Content Studio plays a key role in embedding design excellence directly into model development.

You’ll collaborate with researchers, ML engineers, product managers, and designers to ensure Canva’s visual AI feels premium, human-centered, and unmistakably Canva.

Check out lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know

We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

Please note that interviews are conducted virtually.

Company Description


Nine is Australia’s largest locally owned media company. Working at Nine, you’ll have access to a unique range of experiences and opportunities, helping drive the success of the country’s most trusted television, radio, digital and publishing brands. 

Our content reaches almost every Australian - meaning what we do has real impact. We bring people together, celebrate the big moments, and capture the everyday ones.

Some of our most beloved brands have been part of Australian life for generations, and others - new on the scene - have already found their place firmly in our lives. We’re evolving and we need people like you to bring new ideas, innovate and make your mark.

Job Description


The Data Science & AI team develops, builds, and delivers production-grade AI products to provide solutions to business problems. Key responsibilities include architecting scalable software, building agentic workflows, and managing the ops and pipelines used to deploy them. We push the envelope in what is possible and work on new and novel implementations of AI engineering.
 
Areas of focus for the team include leveraging LLMs to assist our content creation stakeholders, architecting enterprise-grade semantic search engines, and building agentic workflows that transform raw data into actionable business operations.


Day to day you will:

Architect, maintain and deploy scalable and robust production-grade AI applications and microservices.
Champion utilising non-deterministic flows to drive measurable business outcomes.
Design advanced retrieval architectures (RAG) and optimise LLM orchestration for latency and cost.
Build robust APIs to integrate AI agents with internal databases and third-party tools.
Evaluate agent's performance using evaluations and tests to iterate on prompts, agent tools and agents orchestration to improve output quality and reliability.
Manage the observability and logging of deployed agents to proactively identify and resolve edge cases.
Champion engineering best practices and stay ahead of the rapidly evolving GenAI landscape.

Qualifications


What you’ll bring:

Strong programming skills in Python with a recent focus on building scalable production grade AI applications.
Proven experience with orchestration frameworks (ADK preferred) and implementing RAG architectures with Vector Databases.
Understanding of software design, service architecture, and best practices for scalable systems.
A proven track record of prior production-grade projects utilising prompt engineering & system design with considerations for latency and user experience.

Bonus

Master's degree in Computer Science or AI.
Deep understanding of foundational models and fine-tuning techniques.
Knowledge of evaluation frameworks (e.g., RAGAS, TruLens) or "LLM-as-a-judge" patterns.
Familiarity with Google Cloud Platform (Vertex AI) and containerisation (Docker/Kubernetes).

Additional Information


How we work

At Nine, our flexible work options vary by role and team. Depending on the position, this may include flexible hours, hybrid work, or part-time arrangements. We welcome discussing your flexibility needs during the hiring process - just ask the Talent Acquisition team. 

Our employee benefits include:

18 weeks paid parental leave with no distinction between primary and secondary carers.
Access to 'Employee Exclusives' program - a way of getting closer to our incredible brands, offering unique experiences, behind-the-scenes access, and awesome perks.
Digital newspaper subscription to our mastheads.
Annual gift voucher for Stan subscription.

More info at Nine Careers.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:

We're committed to a safe, respectful and inclusive Nine. From day one, you'll be encouraged to bring your whole self to work and will be supported to perform at your best.

We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with disabilities, and of all ages, genders, nationalities, backgrounds and cultures as we recognise the importance and value of diverse perspectives. Should you require any adjustments to the recruitment process, please advise us when you apply.

Work rights: Please note to apply for this role you must already have the right to lawfully work and live in Australia.

Firstly, what we offer you! 

Vibrant Open Office in Richmond. Work in a dynamic, collaborative space that sparks creativity 

Work Your Way. Enjoy the perfect balance of remote flexibility and in-office collaboration—get the best of both worlds

Make a Difference. Take a paid day off each year to volunteer for a cause you’re passionate about

Fuel Your Day. Enjoy free 7-Eleven coffee and snacks in the office—because great ideas start with great coffee

Family Comes First. Get up to 15 weeks of paid parental leave for both primary and secondary carer, so you can focus on what matters most

Stay Social & Connected. Join our Social Club and Open Committee for regular events, celebrations, and fun activities

Grow Without Limits. Access unlimited LinkedIn Learning courses and invest in your personal and professional development

Duties & Responsibilities

The Data Engineer will be responsible for designing and building reliable data pipelines and supporting our cloud data platform to enable analytics, reporting and operational insights.

Responsibilities include:

Designing, developing and maintaining scalable data pipelines that transform and integrate data from multiple sources

Building solutions that utilise modern cloud data platforms and data pipelines to provide actionable insights into operational efficiency and key business performance metrics

Developing and maintaining batch and streaming data pipelines using modern data engineering frameworks and cloud technologies

Supporting business stakeholders, SMEs, project teams and business analysts in the design, development and delivery of data solutions

Collaborating with data engineers, analysts and other technology teams to implement robust and maintainable data solutions

Developing, managing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines for cloud-hosted systems and applications to support automated deployments

Monitoring and improving the reliability, performance and quality of data pipelines and data platform components

Collaborating with SAP and non-SAP technical teams to define technical specifications and support system integrations

Working with third-party vendors and internal teams to resolve issues and deliver agreed outcomes in a timely manner

Experience

Our ideal candidate will be tertiary qualified within an IT-related discipline and will have demonstrated experience working in a similar role.

You will have excellent communication skills and the ability to develop strong working relationships across teams, as well as with external vendors.

You will also be able to demonstrate the following:

Hands-on experience working with cloud-based data platforms, preferably Microsoft Azure

Experience working with cloud data services such as Azure SQL Database, Azure Data Lake, Azure Data Factory, Azure Synapse or Azure Databricks

Strong SQL skills with experience developing data-intensive solutions

Experience building and maintaining data pipelines and ETL/ELT processes

Experience working with distributed data processing frameworks such as Apache Spark or Databricks

Experience with at least one programming language, preferably Python

Strong understanding of data modelling concepts and best practices

Experience working with CI/CD pipelines and modern DevOps practices

Experience working in collaborative Agile delivery environments

Knowledge of Power BI or other analytics tools is desirable but not essential

Ad Text:
About Us:
We are a relationship-driven Managed Services Provider focused on high-quality service and long-term client partnerships. Our culture is built on trust, autonomy and accountability, with minimal bureaucracy.

We’re now looking for an engineer who enjoys improving systems, automating repetitive work and building a genuinely proactive managed services platform.

Position Overview:
This role owns the technical platforms that power our proactive services – monitoring, patching, automation and backup assurance.

You’ll have real ownership of the tooling and the opportunity to design and improve how our managed services operate.

Key Responsibilities:

Own and optimise our RMM platform

Improve monitoring and alerting to reduce noise and surface real issues

Design and maintain structured patch management across client environments

Build automation to eliminate repetitive service desk work

Improve integration between RMM, PSA, Microsoft 365 and security tooling

Ensure client backups are monitored, tested and reliable

Develop documentation, standards and automation playbooks

This role is ideal for an engineer who enjoys improving systems rather than just reacting to tickets.

Technical Environment:

Microsoft 365 / Azure

Windows Server environments

RMM platform management

Backup platforms (e.g. Veeam)

PowerShell and automation tooling

Endpoint monitoring and patching systems

Experience We’re Looking For:

MSP experience (preferred)

Experience administering or improving an RMM platform

Strong patch management experience

PowerShell or scripting for automation

Microsoft 365 and Windows Server administration

Experience tuning monitoring alerts and reducing noise

Backup monitoring and restore testing

Nice to Have:

Security alert triage experience

EDR / XDR exposure

Automation and API integrations between tools

Why This Role Is Different:

This is not a narrow ticket-processing role. You’ll have real ownership over the systems that make our managed services proactive, and the freedom to improve them. If you enjoy building automation, reducing noise, and improving service delivery platforms, this role will suit you.

What You’ll Get:

Real ownership of tooling and standards

Opportunity to shape the proactive services platform

A high-trust engineering environment

Genuine work-life balance

Competitive salary with room to grow

How to Apply:

Please include:

The RMM platforms you have administered

Examples of automation or monitoring improvements you’ve implemented

Your experience with backup monitoring or restore testing

At TechnologyOne, our mission is straightforward: to simplify life for our customers and empower them to thrive in a rapidly changing world. We are seeking a Senior Python Developer - Data & AI with deep experience in data-driven and AI-powered systems, particularly to work on agentic and LLM-based architectures. This role focuses on designing, building, and deploying intelligent automation and AI agents that operate reliably in production environments.

You will work closely with product, data, and engineering leaders to deliver AI solutions that create tangible business impact. This is a hands-on senior role with strong technical ownership and influence over standards, architecture, and best practices.


What you'll be doing...

AI & Agentic Development



Design, build, and maintain agentic AI systems (multi-step reasoning, tool use, orchestration, memory, and state management).
Develop LLM-powered workflows for automation, decision-making, and user-facing AI interactions.
Implement prompt strategies, function/tool calling, and structured outputs for production-grade AI systems.
Evaluate and integrate AI frameworks and libraries (e.g. LangGraph-style flows, custom orchestration).

Data-Focused Engineering



Work extensively with structured and unstructured data (JSON, text, tabular datasets).
Build data processing and transformation logic to support AI workflows and analytics.
Design efficient data access patterns for AI inference and evaluation.
Ensure data quality, traceability, and observability across AI pipelines.

Technical Leadership



Set coding standards and best practices for Python and AI development.
Review code and mentor junior developers in AI and data engineering principles.
Provide architectural guidance on AI systems and platform evolution.
Contribute to technical documentation and internal knowledge sharing.


Your Talents…

Core Technical Skills



5+ years of professional Python development experience.
Strong experience working with data-centric applications.
Hands-on experience building and deploying AI / ML or LLM-based systems.
Solid understanding of agentic concepts (planning, tools, memory, state).
Experience with APIs, asynchronous processing, and event-driven systems.

AI & Data Stack



Experience with LLMs.
Familiarity with vector embeddings, retrieval, and similarity search.
Experience working with AI evaluation, prompt iteration, and model behaviour tuning.
Strong SQL and data analysis skills.

Engineering Practices



Experience shipping production systems, not just experiments.
Strong testing discipline and code quality mindset.
Comfortable working in cloud environments (AWS, Azure, or GCP).
Experience with CI/CD, containerisation, and modern dev workflows.

Why Join us?

TechnologyOne is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer who values diversity and an inclusive workplace. We aim to recruit a diverse range of people with a diverse range of talents to help us to deliver on our mission to better our community. We pride ourselves on providing our people with earned recognition through talent mapping and career progression, competitive salaries and having a supportive tribe environment. Our core values and culture unleash the boundless potential of creativity, innovation and the entrepreneurial spirit within our people. When you experience Life at TechnologyOne you'll see that our team enjoys an extensive array of benefits, including foundation charity activities, which all cultivate a vibrant workplace our people are very proud to be involved with.


Benefits…



Competitive remuneration package
Access to our industry leading employee share plan
Salary packaging options
Up to 10 days of additional leave via our Purchase Leave program
Amazing Culture: 8 x Australian Business Awards "Employer of Choice" winner
We love our team events & celebrate diversity
Flexible hours: we are family-friendly and value life outside of work
Free gym membership in your region (or, if you are located at HQ, we have a new state of the art Gym onsite, exclusive and available 24/7).
Additional 2.5 days of leave per year dedicated to volunteering at a charity of your choice
Free breakfast and coffee on-site
Accelerate your growth with full access to LinkedIn Learning and a clear career framework that maps your path to success.



Who we are…

TechnologyOne (ASX: TNE) is Australia's largest enterprise software company and one of Australia's top 50 ASX-listed companies, with locations across six countries. We provide a global SaaS ERP solution that transforms business and makes life simple for our customers. Our deeply integrated enterprise SaaS solution is available on any device, anywhere and anytime and is incredibly easy to use. Over 1,200 leading corporations, government agencies, local councils and universities are powered by our software. For 38 years, we have been providing our customers with enterprise software that evolves and adapts to new and emerging technologies, allowing them to focus on their business and not technology.

We are seeking a passionate and talented computer vision engineer to work on cutting edge scientific instruments for DNA analysis. The role requires expertise with both classic and machine learning technologies for computer vision.  

We will consider all candidates from graduates with strong skills through to experienced engineers.  The position is based on site at our facility at Yatala - only 25 minutes from Brisbane.   

You will be working with a small team of engineers and scientists to develop computer vision algorithms to improve the performance and reliability of the Myra Liquid Handling System robotic platform (www.biomolecularsystems.com/myra).  

Highly desirable skills include:

Machine learning classification techniques including CNNs

Image processing and classification

Familiarity with relevant frameworks and libraries (e.g., OpenCV, TensorFlow, PyTorch)

Desktop application development with .NET and WPF

Only Australian citizens and permanent residents will be considered for the role.

The Role

AIM is a deep-tech startup building advanced high power laser systems to protect people from weaponised drones. We are seeking a highly motivated and skilled Optical Engineer. At AIM, you will be an integral part in the Engineering and R&D team to develop leading edge products and systems for use in tomorrow’s defence technology. We are looking for candidates who are flexible and available to work outside of ordinary hours based on business needs at our Melbourne facility.


What You Will Do

• Take ownership of laser beam director and imaging subsystems

• Design and prototype high performance optical systems for kW level laser beam propagation and tracking.

• Specify and evaluate imaging lenses within the visible and infrared and thermal wavelength ranges

• Test and evaluate optical components and lenses for diffraction limited performance

• Work with suppliers to manufacture custom optical elements and lenses and coatings

• Work with in-house prototype and manufacturing team to design housings and optical assemblies.

• Interface with hardware and software teams to define and support optical imaging and tracking systems. Provide technical support and mentoring.

• Assist in preparing work estimates and project schedules for work to be performed.

• Work as part of a multi-disciplinary fast paced development team

• Provide leadership to other team members

• Work closely with managers, engineers, and technicians to produce a wide variety of components

‍

What You Will Bring

• Knowledge and hands on experience with free space Gaussian beam propagation

• Experience designing, testing and assembling optical systems

• Experience with optical CAD (Zemax, Code-V/LightTools etc.)

• Have excellent time management skills as you will need to be able to prioritise workloads and shift focus as necessary.

• Experience doing the detailed analysis necessary to make data driven decisions

• Ability to work cross-functionally to develop new solutions

• Able to work well under pressure while managing competing demands and tight deadlines

• Work efficiently and productively in independent and team settings

• Exemplary verbal and written communication skills

• Strong organisational skills with meticulous attention to detail

• Candidates must be an Australian citizen

• Candidates must be able to acquire a Negative Vetting Level 1 (NV1) Security Clearance


How You Will Benefit

• Competitive remuneration

• Exciting projects and industry leading innovations

• Professional learning and development opportunities

• Opportunity to innovate and work in a fast-paced team

• Challenges at the edge of what’s possible

We are committed to ensuring diversity; inclusion and equality are embedded throughout our organisation for the benefit of our customers and our team. We strive for a positive and engaging workplace where mental health and wellbeing are supported. We welcome applicants from all diverse backgrounds, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people. If you are looking for a unique and exciting opportunity, we look forward to your application.

About Aurizn

We are an Australian owned Defence Technology company pushing the limits to support Australian and allied forces deliver a more secure future.

Shape the world at Aurizn to make a real impact and join our team. A recognised Great Place to Work, Aurizn people are driven by our values; Think Clever, Always Deliver, Embrace the Unknown and Be Better Together.

With an industry-leading suite of benefits including ongoing training and growth opportunities, a variety of leave types, health and wellness offerings, flexible working and more, a career with Aurizn empowers you to perform at your best and push the limits for a more secure future.

Shape the world at Aurizn.

The Opportunity

Due to continued growth and a strong pipeline of projects, Aurizn is seeking Data Scientists across all levels of experience to register their interest in joining our talent pool for upcoming opportunities.

This is a great opportunity to be considered for future roles as new projects are secured and our team continues to expand.

The Role

We are seeking a Scientist with strong foundations in Machine Learning and AI to support the development, testing and delivery of data‑driven solutions. This role is ideal for someone who is eager to build skills, contribute to meaningful defence projects, and work alongside experienced practitioners.

Responsibilities, include, but are not limited to:

Assist in developing, testing, and maintaining AI/ML models for real‑world applications.
Analyse and visualise data to generate insights into the systems and simulations.
Identify research challenges and choosing appropriate machine learning methods to enhance decision-making processes.
Choose relevant features and prepare data sets for training, validation, and testing of machine learning models.
Contribute to validation, documentation, and integration activities.
Collaborate with engineers and other data scientists to support successful project delivery.

What We Are Seeking

Active NV1 security clearance is required.
A degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related field.
Early career experience with Python and modern ML frameworks, either through work or postgraduate research.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, and a willingness to learn and grow in a collaborative environment.
Experience in secure or regulated environments, or familiarity with competency frameworks, is beneficial but not essential.

Inaugural AI leadership role to execute AI Strategy and scale AI investment
Methodically scale AI capability on industry leading AI platforms and continue innovative AI adoption (as recognised by Gartner and Microsoft)
Brisbane-based leadership role with flexible work options

Partner with business and ICT teams to identify opportunities and deliver outcomes

Build AI capability, literacy and ways of working across the organisation

 

 

About you

Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing capability across technical and non-technical groups
Experience translating business problems into prioritised AI use cases and value outcomes
Working knowledge of responsible AI, governance and risk management
A collaborative leadership style with a focus on capability uplift and continuous improvement
Tertiary qualifications in IT, Data Science, Statistics, Applied Mathematics or a related field (or equivalent experience) are required. Post-graduate qualifications and relevant certifications are desirable

Proficient in C++.

Leadership potential and mentoring capability.

Humble, collaborative, and analytical mindset.

Willingness to work onsite.

Work in a collaborative, high-performance engineering team.

Company: KBR - Kellogg Brown & Root Pty Ltd
Ad Text:
About KBR:




At KBR – We do things that matter. 

We deliver science, technology and engineering solutions to governments and companies around the world. KBR employs approximately 38,000 people worldwide with customers in more than 80 countries and operations in over 29 countries. 




KBR is proud to work with its customers across the globe to provide technology, value-added services, and long-term operations and maintenance services to ensure consistent delivery with predictable results. At KBR, We Deliver. 




Think.KBR.com




KBR in Australia 

With over 65 years working on some of Australia’s largest and most complex projects, KBR has unmatched experience supporting the nation’s critical infrastructure, energy transition and national security priorities. KBR has around 2,000 employees throughout Australia, who are focused on delivering innovative technology and engineering solutions for a safer, more secure and sustainable future.




Belong, Connect and Grow at KBR 

At KBR, we are passionate about our people and our Zero Harm culture. These inform all that we do and are at the heart of our commitment to, and ongoing journey toward being a People First company. That commitment is central to our team of team’s philosophy and fosters an environment where everyone can Belong, Connect and Grow. We Deliver – Together.




The Opportunity




KBR is advancing a Digital Engineering strategy to help Defence manage complexity and deliver capability faster. Through advanced modelling environments, digital integration facilities, and collaborative engineering platforms, we support the design and integration of complex Defence systems.




As a Systems Architect, you will play a key role in shaping and delivering digital engineering solutions across major Defence programs. Working closely with engineers, architects, and Defence stakeholders, you will develop system architectures, mission engineering models, and digital simulations that support the full systems engineering lifecycle.




Using Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) approaches and tools such as CATIA Cameo, Enterprise Architect or ANSYS, you will capture operational concepts, model mission scenarios, and ensure traceability from requirements through to verification.




You will also contribute to the growth of KBR’s Digital Engineering capability, supporting the development of integration laboratories and collaborative modelling environments. This is an exciting opportunity to work with emerging technologies such as digital twins, modelling and simulation, and advanced digital engineering platforms.




Responsibilities

The key responsibilities of the role will include, but are not limited to:

Develop and maintain system architectures and MBSE models for Defence programs.

Model mission threads, operational concepts and system behaviours.

Define end-to-end architecture solutions aligned with program requirements and standards.

Integrate and validate models from suppliers and project teams within broader system architectures.

Support the development of KBR’s Digital Engineering environments and capabilities.

Collaborate with Defence stakeholders and industry partners to shape architecture strategies and technical roadmaps.

Manage configuration and data across digital engineering toolchains.

Produce architecture artefacts, modelling outputs and technical documentation.




Qualifications, Skills and Experience




Essential:

Degree in Systems Engineering, Computer Science, or related discipline.

10+ years’ experience in systems engineering, systems architecture, or digital engineering roles.

Experience developing system architectures in Defence, aerospace or regulated environments.

Strong understanding of systems engineering lifecycle and MBSE methodologies (SysML/UML).

Experience developing digital models and simulations.

Ability to translate operational concepts into architectural and modelling artefacts.

Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills.

Familiarity with architecture frameworks such as DoDAF, MODAF or NAF.

Eligibility to obtain an Australian Government Security Clearance (NV1 or higher).




Desirable:

Experience with digital engineering environments, modelling and simulation platforms.

Knowledge of Agile, DevOps or modern digital engineering practices.

Familiarity with Python or other scripting languages.

Experience integrating complex software and hardware systems.




Benefits of KBR

A workplace culture certified as a Great Place To Work (Aus, India, UK & US)

Flexible working conditions

Competitive salary (including annual reviews)

Paid Parental leave

Paid Reservist leave

Income protection

Corporate rewards

Salary packaging/Novated leasing

Discounted employee stock purchase plans

Flu shots, skin checks and private health insurance discounts

Career development: Online learning, mentorship and career pathways




If you’re ready to shape tomorrow, let’s get started. Apply Now!




KBR acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country throughout Australia and their continuing connections to land, sea, community and culture. We pay our respects to Elders past and present.




As a Major Service Provider of the Australian Defence Force, an AGSVA security clearance will be required and compliance to International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR). As such, our hiring decisions are based on the key requirements of each role and candidates are selected based on their unique strengths and experiences.

Ad Text:
Senior Systems Engineer (L3) - Microsoft, Azure & Infrastructure
Escalations | Projects | Solution Design | Future AI Engineering Path

Join a high-performing team where you’ll solve complex technical challenges, lead escalations, deliver modern cloud projects, and help design solutions that transform how organisations use technology.

Most IT roles keep you stuck doing tickets.

This one doesn’t.

At Digital Armour, we are building one of Australia’s most ambitious AI-first technology consultancies, helping organisations unlock 20-40% productivity gains through AI, cybersecurity, cloud and modern workplace transformation.


We don’t just “support IT”.

To support our growth, we’re looking for a Senior Systems Engineer (L3) who wants to operate at a higher level - solving complex problems, mentoring others, designing solutions, and helping organisations modernise their technology stack.

If you’re the person everyone calls when things get complicated - keep reading.




What This Role Actually Looks Like

This is not just a support role.

You will operate across three high-impact areas:




Escalation & Technical Leadership

You’ll act as the senior escalation point for our service desk, helping resolve complex technical issues across cloud, infrastructure, networking and security.

You’ll also help mentor and uplift our L1/L2 engineers, improving the overall capability of the team.

 

Projects & Modernisation

You’ll play a key role delivering client projects including:

• Microsoft 365 and Azure migrations
• Infrastructure modernisation
• Cloud architecture improvements
• Security uplift initiatives
• Networking and infrastructure upgrades




Presales & Solution Design

You’ll collaborate with our consulting and presales team to:

• Design modern Microsoft and cloud solutions
• Provide technical input into proposals
• Help shape the right architecture for clients

You’ll be involved in the strategy behind the solution - not just implementing it.





The Technology Environment

Our clients typically run a mix of modern cloud and traditional infrastructure.

You’ll need strong capability across:

Microsoft Platform
• Microsoft 365
• Azure
• Intune / Endpoint Management
• Entra ID / Identity
• SharePoint / collaboration


Infrastructure
• Windows Server environments
• Virtualisation platforms
• Storage and compute environments


Networking
• SD-WAN and modern networking
• Switching and routing
• WiFi infrastructure


Security
• Microsoft security stack
• Firewalls (Sophos, Meraki, Cisco or similar)
• Cybersecurity best practices

You don’t need to know everything - but you must learn fast and solve problems confidently.





What Makes Digital Armour Different

Most MSPs promise culture. We actually build it intentionally.




We operate on four core values:

Integrity & Accountability
We do the right thing and own outcomes.

Excellence
Good is never good enough. We raise the bar constantly.

Continuous Learning
Technology moves fast - we move faster.

Authenticity
No politics. No egos. Just honest collaboration.




We hire people who want to grow, improve and build something meaningful together.





Career Growth (This Role Is a Launchpad)

For the right person, this role opens doors into:

• Technical Architecture
• AI and automation engineering
• Cloud solution design
• Consulting and advisory work
• Technical leadership

We are heavily investing in AI transformation for our clients, and the engineers who join us now will be at the forefront of that shift.

If you want a career that stays relevant for the next decade - this is where it starts.





What We’re Looking For

You’ll likely have:

• 5+ years experience in managed services or complex IT environments
• Strong Microsoft 365 and Azure skills
• Experience supporting infrastructure and networking environments
• Confidence handling escalations and complex troubleshooting
• The ability to communicate clearly with both engineers and clients

But more important than any certification is this:

You care about doing great work and improving constantly.





Why Join Digital Armour

• Work with high-performing engineers who care about quality
• Exposure to modern Microsoft and AI technologies
• Real career growth - not empty promises
• Supportive leadership and strong team culture
• Opportunities to work on meaningful transformation projects


And most importantly:

You’ll be surrounded by people who push each other to get better every week.





Ready for the Next Level?

If you’re looking for a role where:

• your skills are respected
• your growth is supported
• and your work actually matters




We’d love to hear from you.

Apply now and join the Digital Armour Dream Team.
